pr.title: Fix itbox issues, mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Fixed remaining itbox matching issues and changed src/melee/it/items/itbox.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The diff primarily reorders the item state table into the expected data position, swaps the box accessory cleanup call from efLib_DestroyAll to grKongo_801D8058 with the corresponding include change, and adjusts MAX_ROT_VEL to a more exact floating-point literal. The decomp.dev bot reported one new GALE01 match for main/melee/it/items/itbox .data, from 0.00% to 100.00%, plus a .sdata2 improvement from 94.12% to 98.04%. No PR body or human review feedback was present in the slice.

key_files.role: Main item source update: state table placement, cleanup call correction, include change, and floating-point constant adjustment.
Marks melee/it/items/itbox.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.

what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itbox.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Removed include of ef/eflib.h and added include of gr/grkongo.h in itbox.c.

Moved/introduced ItemStateTable it_803F5850[] near the top of itbox.c before itBox_Logic1_Spawned and itBox_Logic1_Destroyed, defining nine motion-state entries using itBox_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks.
Relocated itBox_Logic1_Spawned and itBox_Logic1_Destroyed after it_80286088 in source order.
Changed itBox_Logic1_Destroyed cleanup for ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj from efLib_DestroyAll(...) to grKongo_801D8058(...), then still nulls spawned_gobj.
Bot-reported result: itbox .data became a new 100% match, adding 144 matched data bytes; itbox .sdata2 improved by 4 bytes.
smart_moves: The fix targeted both symbol behavior and binary layout: the state table placement was important for .data matching, not just code readability.

Replacing efLib_DestroyAll with grKongo_801D8058 suggests the spawned accessory cleanup path needed the stage/ground-specific function used by the original binary, and the include was updated accordingly.
The tiny MAX_ROT_VEL literal correction shows attention to exact double/float constant identity, which is often necessary for .sdata2 matching.
Only after the targeted fixes were made was the object flipped to Matching in configure.py, tying the build configuration change to verified match output.
decomp_lessons: For item files, source-order placement of ItemStateTable data can be decisive for .data matching; do not assume tables can be placed anywhere without affecting match status.
When a cleanup function is semantically plausible but not matching, compare the called subsystem carefully. Here the matching call was grKongo_801D8058 rather than efLib_DestroyAll.
Micro differences in numeric literals can block or reduce .sdata2 matching; preserve exact recovered constants such as 0.05235987715423107.
When marking a unit as Matching, include the configure.py status flip in the same PR as the evidence-producing source fixes.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for validating data-section work: this PR's measurable win was .data 0.00% to 100.00% for main/melee/it/items/itbox.
naming_conventions: Item logic callbacks use the existing itBox_Logic1_Spawned and itBox_Logic1_Destroyed naming pattern.
Unknown motion callbacks remain in the itBox_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll style, reflecting unresolved but consistently grouped item state behavior.
The item state table retains its address-style generated name it_803F5850.
Item variable access follows the existing field path ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj and ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.opened.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reorder data declarations to match the binary's expected .data layout before changing build status to Matching.
Use exact floating-point literals for constants involved in matched .sdata2 output; here MAX_ROT_VEL required 0.05235987715423107.
Check callee identity for destructor/cleanup routines, since a same-signature or semantically similar helper can still fail matching.
Use decomp.dev output to confirm section-level effects: .data reached 100.00%, while .sdata2 improved but was still reported as 98.04% for the unit.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed linked code +5072 bytes, matched data +144 bytes, linked data +264 bytes; one new match for main/melee/it/items/itbox .data from 0.00% to 100.00%, and .sdata2 improvement from 94.12% to 98.04%.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.

follow_up_queries: Find other item files where ItemStateTable declaration order affects .data matching.
Search for efLib_DestroyAll calls in item destructors that may need a more specific cleanup routine.
Compare grKongo_801D8058 usage across item accessory or spawned_gobj cleanup paths.
Audit remaining .sdata2 mismatches in itbox after MAX_ROT_VEL was corrected.
